programme.desc: Chronicling Operation Carpetbagger, one of the most top-secret missions of World War II.

programme.title: Battle of Okinawa: Operation Iceberg
programme.desc: Insight into the Battle of Okinawa, the largest assault of the Pacific campaign.

programme.title: Dogfight Over Guadalcanal
programme.desc: The true story of two pilots, one American and the other Japanese, who confronted each other during a key WWII battle and, for some mysterious reason, did not shoot each other down.

programme.title: Victory Remembered, Legacy of The Black Devils
programme.desc: Filmmaker Les Owen travels with former Black Devils and their families to the mountains, beaches and villages of Italy and France to share stories of their bravery, commitment and victories in the face of overwhelming odds.

programme.title: Ten Days to Victory
programme.desc: The unique stories of ten very different people who were caught up in the liberation of Europe from the grip of Nazi terror are interwoven .

programme.title: Ten Days to Victory
programme.desc: The stories of 10 very different people who were caught up in the liberation of Europe from the grip of Nazi Germany during the Second World War.

programme.title: World War II in Colour
programme.desc: In the run-up to September 1939, political and economic chaos in the aftermath of World War I allowed Hitler in Germany, Mussolini in Italy and ultra-nationalists in imperial Japan to seize absolute power.

programme.title: World War II in Colour
programme.desc: Germany took just four weeks to conquer Poland, using a new form of warfare - Blitzkrieg. Six armoured tank units known as Panzer divisions, supported by the Luftwaffe, quickly broke through Polish defences.

programme.title: World War II in Colour
programme.desc: After the retreat from Dunkirk, Churchill rallied Britain in its darkest hour. Hitler was only just across the channel, but he reckoned without our supreme strategist, the might of the navy and the manoeuvrability of the air force.

programme.title: World War II in Colour
programme.desc: Explores Hitlers assault on the Soviet Union in the summer of 1941, Operation Barbarossa and his attack on the Caucasus in 1942.

programme.title: Heinrich Muller - Head of the Gestapo
programme.desc: Heinrich Muller, WWI veteran and Freikorps member, rose to power and aligned with Adolf Hitler.

programme.title: Hitlers Lost Battles
programme.desc: A look at the strategic decisions made by Hitler during the Battle of Britain, which led to Operation Barbarossa, and the Axis invasion of the Soviet Union.

programme.title: Hitlers Lost Battles
programme.desc: The poor military decisions made by Hitler started to become clear by 1942. America got involved in the conflict as a reaction to his declaration of war, as they made ground in North Africa.

programme.title: Great American Generals - Patton, MacArthur, Eisenhower, Bradley
programme.desc: A compilation of archive footage provides insight into what made Patton, MacArthur, Eisenhower, and Bradley heroes of American history.

programme.title: World War II in Colour
programme.desc: The attack on Pearl Harbour brought the sleeping giant, America, into the war, and saw the emergence of the aircraft carrier as the pre-eminent weapon of the seas.

programme.title: World War II in Colour
programme.desc: Mussolini wanted to recreate the Roman Empire, but proved to be a poor military strategist and his incompetence contributed to Hitlers eventual defeat.

programme.title: World War II in Colour
programme.desc: With their armies fully engaged on the battlegrounds, the Allied and Axis powers focused their efforts to develop new weapons and new strategies to deliver a knockout blow to the enemy and bring about a quick end to the conflict.

programme.title: World War II in Colour
programme.desc: In the run-up to September 1939, political and economic chaos in the aftermath of World War I allowed Hitler in Germany, Mussolini in Italy and ultra-nationalists in imperial Japan to seize absolute power.

programme.title: World War II in Colour
programme.desc: Germany took just four weeks to conquer Poland, using a new form of warfare - Blitzkrieg. Six armoured tank units known as Panzer divisions, supported by the Luftwaffe, quickly broke through Polish defences.

programme.title: World War II in Colour
programme.desc: After the retreat from Dunkirk, Churchill rallied Britain in its darkest hour. Hitler was only just across the channel, but he reckoned without our supreme strategist, the might of the navy and the manoeuvrability of the air force.

programme.title: World War II in Colour
programme.desc: After the retreat from Dunkirk, Churchill rallied Britain in its darkest hour. Hitler was only just across the channel, but he reckoned without our supreme strategist, the might of the navy and the manoeuvrability of the air force.

programme.title: Ross Kemp: Shipwreck Treasure Hunter
programme.desc: After hearing about his great-grandfather who served in the Merchant Navy, Ross Kemp is inspired to explore the wreck of a WWII cargo ship and try to recover its military supplies.

programme.title: Cracking the Code
programme.sub-title: The Code That Won the Great War
programme.desc: Uncover some of the worlds most renowned encryptions, revealing how they were decoded, the minds who cracked them and the secrets they masked.

programme.title: Secrets of Great British Castles
programme.desc: Built by William the Conqueror, the Tower of London is one of Britains most iconic landmarks. It has been a military fortress, a palace, a royal mint, a prison, a zoo and a place of execution, a silent witness to some of the most momentous events in our history. Dan Jones reveals some of its fascinating secrets and tells the stories of the traitors, rebels and royalty who met their end inside its imposing walls.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Dr Alice Roberts follows an entire year of British archaeology, joining up the results of digs and investigations the length of the country. The results are astonishing, and sometimes disturbing. Roman finds include the mystery of 97 babies murdered by the Thames, a fabulous Roman coin hoard found in Somerset and a man buried on a layer of dead animals.

programme.title: Britains Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: Alice is in Lincoln, getting to grips with life in medieval England. She visits the magnificent cathedral that was once the tallest building in the world and re-lives the battle at Lincoln Castle.

programme.title: Britains Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: Alice is in London, as she attempts to make sense of the world-changing events during the short but eventful Restoration period. She learns the violent and vengeful truth behind Charles IIs carefully crafted public image.

programme.title: Shipwrecks: When History Resurfaces
programme.desc: From the Indian Oceans clear blue atolls to the icy waters of the St. Lawrence River, an epic expedition with the Cinemarine team to capture the remains of six of the worlds most spectacular underwater disasters.

programme.title: Secrets of Great British Castles
programme.desc: Dan Jones explores the turbulent history of Carrickfergus, one of the most ancient castles in Northern Ireland and a stronghold that holds the key to understanding the tempestuous relationship between Britain and Ireland.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: The team are on the hunt for archaeology from the more recent past as they follow the search for artefacts from World War II. They join marine archaeologists in the Solent as they raise the once-in-a-lifetime find of a Fairey Barracuda divebomber. More than 2,500 Barracudas were in service during the war but not a single complete plane survives today. Naoíse Mac Sweeney joins the post excavation to reveal how the find could help bring this rare aircraft back to life.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Dr Alice Roberts follows an entire year of British archaeology, joining up the results of digs and investigations the length of the country. The results are astonishing, and sometimes disturbing. Roman finds include the mystery of 97 babies murdered by the Thames, a fabulous Roman coin hoard found in Somerset and a man buried on a layer of dead animals.

programme.title: Secrets of Great British Castles
programme.desc: Dan is in the heart of the English countryside at Warwick Castle. While it appears idyllic today, for centuries it was a poisoned chalice to the Earls of Warwick who lived there and its walls echo with tales of betrayal, bloodshed and murder.

programme.title: Secrets of Great British Castles
programme.desc: Historian Dan Jones is in the heart of Snowdonia exploring a stronghold built not only as an impenetrable fortress but also as a lavish royal palace fit for a fairytale - Caernarfon Castle.

programme.title: Britains Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: In Cheltenham, Britains most Regency town, Professor Alice Roberts attends a period society ball, investigates social hierarchy and explores the Regency fondness for enemas.

programme.title: Britains Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: In Belfast, the UKs most Victorian city, Alice Roberts visits the shipyards that built the Titanic, gets locked up in the Crumlin Road gaol and learns about 19th-century etiquette.

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: The dramatic moors and valleys of West Yorkshire are the setting for an unusual slice of the past in Tony Robinsons first walk from this new series. This evocative landscape was the home of, and inspiration for, the Brontes, the remarkable literary family that produced Wuthering Heights and Jane Eyre.

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: Tony Robinson visits the Cairngorms National Park to discover how Queen Victoria and Prince Albert helped to shape Scottish traditions and imagery such as tartan, bagpipes and caber tossing.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: A look back at the best archaeological findings of 2014, including the Barrow Clump, Ipplepen and Winchester sites.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Archaeologists search Orkney and the North of Britain, where they discover a Roman altar and a 5,000-year-old temple.

programme.title: Ghost Ships
programme.desc: Marrs Island, Nova Scotia was the site of one of the worst civilian maritime disasters in history, with more than 550 casualties. The team is diving in to search for answers to figure out how this tragic accident came to be.

programme.title: Ghost Ships
programme.desc: For more than 200 years, tales of a Phantom Ship have surrounded the waters of Pictou, Nova Scotia: a mysterious ship engulfs in flames and suddenly vanishes. Recently, a charred wooden wreck was discovered in the middle of Pictou Harbour.

programme.title: Invasion: The Normans
programme.desc: In 12th-century Ireland, provincial kings vie for supremacy. When two of these power players drive a rival into exile, he seeks help from Henry II, the Anglo-Norman king of England. Henrys vassal Strongbow and other Norman allies then invade Ireland with frightening power.

programme.title: Invasion: The Normans
programme.desc: Following the course of the invasion, as Strongbow wins victory after victory. In 1171, Henry II himself arrives to takes control of Ireland, and grants land there to settlers from Britain. Now, archaeology and cutting-edge science reveal their fascinating lives.

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: Tonys walk takes him back to 1940 when the sleepy county of Dorset became the frontline in the war against Hitler. His five-day, 60-mile walk along the Jurassic coast reveals the countys hidden World War II story. Starting by the defences on Chesil Beach, Tonys journey encompasses stunning scenery and amazing acts of ingenuity and bravery as he heads east to finish in Swanage and Studland Bay.

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: Tony Robinson goes for a walk through some of Britains beautiful and historic landscapes. Tony kicks off the series with a 40-mile walk through the glorious Peak District, along the Derwent Valley, where the worlds industrial revolution was born. This is a journey that reveals how Britain transformed itself from a nation of farmers into the industrial powerhouse of the world.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts visits a house in Otterton, Devon, which is given a stunning transformation to match the views from the windows, Lucy Alexander looks round a small one-bedroom flat in Tottenham, London, and Dion Dublin peers into a building in Alvaston, Derby, which is in a dangerous state of disrepair.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts visits a house in Otterton, Devon, which is given a stunning transformation to match the views from the windows, Lucy Alexander looks round a small one-bedroom flat in Tottenham, London, and Dion Dublin peers into a building in Alvaston, Derby, which is in a dangerous state of disrepair.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Lucy Alexander and Dion Dublin visit a one-bedroom flat in Catford, London, a large house near the River Mersey and a semi-detached property in Alvaston in Derby.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: An end-terrace house in Alfreton in Derby bought by two brothers, a mid-terrace on the coast at Seaham in County Durham and a large three-storey property with a flat and shop in Taylorstown in the Rhondda Valley are all sold at auction. Martin Roberts, Lucy Alexander and Dion Dublin talk to the new owners to find out what they have planned for their auction buys.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts falls in love with a quirky cottage in Malvern bought by a builder and his wife, Dion Dublin talks to a first-time property developer who sees her end-terrace house in Chell in Stoke-on-Trent as something for her pension pot, and Lucy Alexander meets a divorced couple who have decided to form a new union as property developers doing up a shop and flat in Dover, Kent.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A large piece of land in Ratby in Leicestershire, a terrace in Chesterton in Staffordshire and a 1930s semi with a huge garden and huge potential are all sold at auction. Martin Roberts, Lucy Alexander and Dion Dublin meet the developers hoping to breathe new life into their auction buys.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A two-bedroom detached house in Basford in Nottingham, a top floor flat in Portsmouth and a large Edwardian terrace in Crewe are all sold under the hammer. Martin, Lucy and Dion meet the new owners and hear about their plans.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A multimillion-pound development in Farningham in Kent, an ambitious, total renovation project in Aspatria in Cumbria and a very small studio flat in Wallington in Surrey are all sold under the hammer. Martin and Lucy meet the new owners to discuss their plans.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A Victorian terrace in Londons Greenwich, two flats in Chatham in Kent and a riverside inn near Great Yarmouth in Norfolk are all sold under the hammer. Martin Roberts, Lucy Alexander and Dion Dublin meet the new owners and hear plans for their renovation projects.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A three-bedroom house in Borrowash in Derbyshire, another three-bedder in West Bromwich in the Midlands and a quirky four-bedroom house on the Isle of Sheppey are all sold under the hammer.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A flat in Pegswood in Northumberland, a terrace in Swadlincote in Derbyshire and a former shop in Chatham in Kent are given makeovers.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Lucy meets a young couple expecting a baby who have to weigh up whether to live in their auction buy in Sydenham in London while Martin talks to a man and his father in law who have bought their first property together in Trentham near Stoke and Dion meets a man who only went into property development after he retired. His latest buy is a three-bed semi in West Bromwich.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A mid-terrace house in Wales, a basement office in central London with a huge price tag and two flats in Bootle near Liverpool are all sold under the hammer.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Terraced houses in Tunstall in Shropshire and Prescott in Liverpool and a large flat in Dagenham are all sold under the hammer. Martin, Lucy and Dion have a look round before hearing what the new owners have planned.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A chiropodist, two part-time gardeners and a woman who works on projects in between the school run are todays investors. Theyve bought property in Bullbridge in Derbyshire, Newcastle under Lyme in Staffordshire and Wolverhampton in the West Midlands under the hammer.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts follows the progress of a couple developing a chalet bungalow in Drakelowe in Staffordshire for their family, while Lucy Alexander talks to a young entrepreneur with grand plans for a three-bed semi in Croydon in London. Dion Dublin looks round a piece of land which runs alongside a tourist steam train track in Coltishall in Norfolk.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts rolls back the clock nearly 12 years to catch up on an ambitious development of a former church in Preston on the Hill in Cheshire, while Lucy Alexander looks round two very different terraced houses in Stoney Stratford, near Milton Keynes, and Streatham in London.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A 1930s terrace in Croydon, a semi in Wolstanton destined to be a family home and the only house on a street in Moorside in Oldham are all under the hammer. Martin, Lucy and Dion visit the properties and meet the new owners.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin visits a stone cottage in Tilton on the Hill in rural Leicestershire while Martin Roberts is at a property auction buy in Croydon on the outskirts of London and Lucy Alexander meets two friends who have bought a bungalow in Rainham in Kent.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A mid-terrace house in Chaddesdon in Derby, a two-bedroom flat in Londons Maida Vale and a property in Warrington are all sold under the hammer. Martin Roberts, Lucy Alexander and Dion Dublin meet the new owners to hear what they have planned for their auction buys.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin looks round a pretty cottage with outbuildings with huge potential in Burton upon Trent, while Lucy checks out a flat in Londons Crystal Palace and Dion is in Mossley near Manchester in a four-bedroom bungalow.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin tackles the issue of solar panels on a property in Mountain Ash in Wales and wonders if itll be a sunny outcome for the new owner, while Lucy looks round a barn in Elstree in Hertfordshire and Dion visits a flat in Newport in Shropshire.

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Emmerdale is one of Britains best-loved and highly-viewed long-running drama soaps - set in the heart of the beautiful countryside of the Yorkshire Dales, this realistic and down-to-earth drama superbly chronicles the joys and the heartbreaks of a tightly-knit countryside community.

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Emmerdale is one of Britains best-loved and highly-viewed long-running drama soaps - set in the heart of the beautiful countryside of the Yorkshire Dales, this realistic and down-to-earth drama superbly chronicles the joys and the heartbreaks of a tightly-knit countryside community.

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: The story of everyday life in a small and tightly-knit community - this is the core of the worlds longest running prime-time drama serial and the highest-rating programme in the UK. Coronation Street has for over 50 years uniquely combined cult status with popularity and durability - the quality of writing is second to none, portraying believable characters with a large helping of humour. Its themes - love, passion, deception, greed and intrigue - are ones which everyone can relate to.

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: Over an August Bank Holiday, an empty Oxford college is the venue for a weekend attended by professional quiz contestants. Lewis and Hathaway are called in when outstanding competitor Ethan Croft is found floating dead in the college fountain. They learn that Croft was an outrageous flirt with a number of secrets, and the pair race to uncover the killer before the gathering breaks up.

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A family passing through the village bring terror to the inhabitants of Aidensfield when their lost dog is discovered to have rabies. Nick and Kate work against the clock to find the missing animal and save the community. Ashfordly Police have a new vehicle, which unfortunately Alf damages on its first time out.

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Nick takes an undercover fishing holiday in Whitby to investigate a case of smuggling, and Ventress proves to have some useful leads. Kate has to break some difficult news to a patient.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Controversial plans to build a supermarket in Elverton-cum-Latterly lead to murder when a man is stabbed to death and the pollution advisor for the site seems to be spending more time with the women of the village than at work. Can Barnaby and Jones discover the truth and learn what is really polluting Elverton?

programme.desc: Follow-up to the historical epic `Zulu, which documents the events preceding the battle in the original film and the political situation at the time. programme.title: Equilibrium
programme.desc: In the near future, following WWIII, the population of the nation of Libria must take the expression-suppressant Prozia II to suppress the emotions their ruler believes caused the war. To prevent violation of the law, enforcement officers called Grammatons punish anyone that shows signs of emotion, but when one of them forgets his dose, he discovers a rebel group who have kept art and literature alive.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole, Simon OBrien and Max McMurdo travel across the UK to find unique items lost in peoples homes to fix up for a profit. Simon and Max visit a house stuck in the 1950s.

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole, Simon OBrien and Max McMurdo travel across the UK to find unique items lost in peoples homes to fix up for a profit. This episode features an old carpenters chest.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Simon White and friend and neighbour Shirley Boyle are first to host at The Old Hall Country House in the picturesque village of Madeley in Cheshire. On arrival most of the guests are awed by the historical 16th century BandB. In the afternoon, Simon and Shirley take their guests tap dancing and in keeping with the five-star luxury to a private fine dining restaurant for dinner.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Its day two in the competition and the turn of cat-lover Eileen Finn to cater for her BandB competitors at the four-star Thornley House in Allendale, Northumbria. After being blown away by first hosts Simon and Shirley at the luxury Old Hall Country House, Eileen has her work cut out. Also, Eileens beloved and very large Maine Coon cats are not to all of her guests liking.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Jessica and Adrian Rashleigh are third to host at their three bedroom establishment Chy Conyn in St Ives, on the beautiful Cornish coast. The hosts hope that what they lack for in size they will make up for in stunning surroundings. For the activity Jessica and Adrian take their guests on a magical mystery camper van tour of the local sights. At breakfast some of the guests struggle with the small breakfast room.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final hosts are Tim and Karen from The Cavendish in Bournemouth. The guests are initially impressed with the 16-bed `pink palace, but things go downhill when Simon and Shirley vigorously test their bed - and break it. Jessica and Adrian are worried that their room, situated right next to the front door, may mean a noisy night. Eileen however is delighted by the feline memorabilia left in her room as a special touch.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The rivals reconvene for one last time to find out how much theyve been paid by their fellow BandB owners, and to sort out their differences. Simon White of Old Hall Country House is stunned by a generous overpayment, but Eileen Finn from the four-star Thornley is on the receiving end of some harsh criticism and some even harsher payments, and a shock underpayment leaves Tim from The Cavendish in Bournemouth reeling.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: First to host is Sara whos hoping to blow her guests away with a menu of Middle Eastern cuisine inspired by her birthplace, Baghdad, in her bid to win the £1000 prize.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: On the second night of the competition in and around Cumbria, bricklayer Dale tries to impress with a menu of construction-themed comfort food. On a poke about their hosts house, guests Vicki and Ash discover Dales hi-vis work trousers.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Council refuse worker Vicki hopes to impress her guests with a hospital-themed evening, serving up `cracked ribs with a blood shot and some bandage-based entertainment.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Theres some Catalan cuisine and a blindfolded cocktail making competition as entrepreneur Ash hosts the penultimate night of the competition hoping to win the £1000 prize.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Its the final night and Rob treats his guests to the delights of some local produce in an attempt to win the grand. Rob serves Cumberland sausage and black pudding for his starter, but some stray eggshell might ruin his chances.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: In Spain, four sets of British expats battle it out to be named best value BandB. At Vista Del Torcal in the Andalucian Mountains, twenty-somethings Ben Eyre and Anna Chalu are the first hosts. While guests are impressed by their rooms, some are less enthusiastic about an afternoon of rock climbing. In the morning, Ben and Anna hope a full English breakfast can save the day after nighttime drama for Julia.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit of the competition is to the Little Gloster on the Isle of Wight, owned and run by 27-year-old professional chef Ben Cooke. Assisting him is his 81-year-old Danish grandmother Lilian, who has over 50 years experience in the trade.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Its the third day of the weeks competition and the turn of Stephen to host at the four-star Old Hall House. Stephen hasnt opened up to the group yet, much to the chagrin of some of the other guests, who seem to have taken a dislike to him.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final visit of the competition is to the House of Agnes in Canterbury, run by ex-hotel inspector Sandie De Rougemont. With her guests now fully aware of her past career, could the bombshell backfire on the self-proclaimed `BandB Buddha?

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Its the final day and all the BandB hosts gather for one last time to find out how much theyve been paid. Who will take the prize of the best value BandB? It falls to the ex-hotel inspector to award the prize.

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Phil Spencer catches up with two couples who were struggling to find suitable first marital homes: Rupreet and Sundeep in Harrow and Paul and Elly in Bristol.

programme.title: Kirstie and Phils Love It or List It
programme.desc: Kirstie catches up with Gary and Bill, who were torn over their higgledy-piggledy cemetery lodge in Edinburgh. But did Kirsties transformation trump Phils finds?

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Adam and Tassy convert a 20-metre-high Northamptonshire water tower into a fun family home with curved walls, slides, secret rooms, a firefighters pole, a swimming pool and a sky garden.

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: Paula, whos 84, has been referred to St Georges by her GP for urgent treatment. A routine blood test has detected that Paula has a dangerously high level of potassium, or salts, in her blood.

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: Doctors have to deal with a nine-year-old Robert whose heart rate is dangerously high. A 32-year-old Mark may have damaged his spine after falling from scaffolding, and a 74-year-old Christine arrives in AandE after an accident in her garden.

programme.title: Emergency Helicopter Medics
programme.desc: The helicopter medics battle bad weather in Cumbria to reach injured 70-year-old hill-walker Jenny. Meanwhile, Scotts attempts at DIY land him in serious trouble and biker Ronald needs urgent treatment after a crash.

programme.title: Emergency Helicopter Medics
programme.desc: Medics fly to the aid of a teenage mountain biker, a multi-vehicle collision in Bedford results in potentially life-changing injuries, and Nicola steps in to save her husband Michaels life when he has a cardiac arrest.

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE

programme.desc: Jimmy Doherty, Kate Quilton and Matt Tebbutt present some of their favourite investigations. Kate heads to Spain in pursuit of the perfect tomato. Jimmy travels to Iceland to investigate Skyr yoghurt. Matt dives for edible seaweed in Cornwall.

programme.desc: A curious, naive, yet friendly little pig navigates through her life with her friends and family by her side. Guided by her loving mother and brash father, Peppa is stuck almost every day. Her little siblings, however, look up to Peppa lovingly.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: A curious, naive, yet friendly little pig navigates through her life with her friends and family by her side. Guided by her loving mother and brash father, Peppa is stuck almost every day. Her little siblings, however, look up to Peppa lovingly.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: On a rainy day, Granny Sheep plays in the garden with Peppa and Suzy. They decide to play with cardboard boxes and build a hospital, a cookie factory, and a train station.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: The children want to make their own takeaway. Together they cook and deliver the pretend takeaways to the parents. They get great reviews, but nothing beats a real lunch!

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppa and George play with walkie-talkies at Granny and Grandpa Pigs house. Grandpa Pig teaches Peppa and George how to use codenames to talk using the gadgets.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: A curious, naive, yet friendly little pig navigates through her life with her friends and family by her side. Guided by her loving mother and brash father, Peppa is stuck almost every day. Her little siblings, however, look up to Peppa lovingly.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: A curious, naive, yet friendly little pig navigates through her life with her friends and family by her side. Guided by her loving mother and brash father, Peppa is stuck almost every day. Her little siblings, however, look up to Peppa lovingly.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: At the playground, Pedro and Pennys glasses fall off; both think that their glasses are broken, as they cannot see very well; Madame Gazelle shows Pedro and Penny different types of objects with lenses.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppa, George, Mummy and Daddy Pig have gone to visit Madame Gazelle. The family soon realise that her house is being pushed over by the tall tree next to it. Mr. Bull arrives and with his help they all manage to save Madame Gazelles House and tree.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Daddy Pig receives a letter saying he must go away on business to test some very important concrete. Peppa, Mummy and George all decide to go with Daddy and enjoy a train journey, where they sing to pass the time and even have a sleep on the train.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Mummy Sheep gets the news that she has got a new job far away, leaving Suzy and Peppa to wonder how theyre going to carry on being best friends; Peppa gives Suzy some presents to remember her by, before a surprising change for Mummy Sheep changes everything.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Its time for a day out and Peppa, George, Mummy Pig, Daddy Pig and Suzy Sheep all go to visit Tiny Land. They see tiny dinosaurs, their favourite tiny landmarks and even a tiny Miss Rabbit doing even more jobs!

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: When Peppa and Suzy learn that grown ups must work all day, they decide to play at working in a shop. They quickly find it is very tiring.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppa and her family spot a rainbow when they go for a drive.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Pedro gets a cough at playschool and the other children soon catch it. Doctor Brownbear is called and gives them horrible medicine, but who will look after him when he is ill?

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Daddy Pig has a book from the library that he has kept for too long. The family go to the library to return it and find more books to read.

programme.title: Ben and Hollys Little Kingdom
programme.desc: Holly invites Ben to the annual Royal Fairy Picnic. King Thistle hopes there wont be a repeat of last years magical jelly flood.

programme.title: Ben and Hollys Little Kingdom
programme.desc: Holly and Ben think Gaston the Ladybird is sad so they try and cheer him up by tidying his cave, but Gaston liked his cave just as it was all messy and untidy.

programme.title: Ben and Hollys Little Kingdom
programme.desc: Holly needs her wand to do magic, but one day Holly loses her wand and is very sad. Ben uses his elf skills to find it again.

programme.title: Ben and Hollys Little Kingdom
programme.desc: Holly goes with Ben and Mr Elf to the elf farm to collect an egg for the Kings breakfast. While there a huge chicken escapes from the farm and Holly and the elves have to chase it.

programme.title: The Adventures of Paddington
programme.sub-title: Paddington and the Big Decision; Paddingtons Beach Stowaway
programme.desc: On the last day of the school holidays, Jonathan and Judy cant agree on what to do; the Browns come back from a beach trip.

programme.title: Gabbys Dollhouse
programme.desc: Gabby finds a book with the same doodle on every page; inside the dollhouse, the Gabby Cats use their imagination to add their own spin to the drawings.

programme.title: Blues Clues and You!
programme.desc: Josh and Blue play a wintry game of Blues Clues to figure out what her surprise is; along the way, they make shapes in the snow with their friends and sing a snow day song with Mo Snow.

programme.title: Circle Square
programme.desc: Douglas Pine is under pressure to win the sunflower growing contest, but he hasnt been listening to his grandfathers top growing hints, so he looks for a handy shortcut to success.

programme.title: Circle Square
programme.desc: Isabella the bear wants to find an exercise that suits her. She heads off with Vanessa to find inspiration with their neighbours.

programme.title: Circle Square
programme.desc: Duster the dogs kennel is taken for repairs, and hes left with a bland replacement. Vanessa appeals to the neighbours to find ways to make his new abode more homely.

programme.title: PAW Patrol
programme.sub-title: Pups Solve a Snap-N-Wrap Problem ; Pups Save Helga and the Dingers
programme.desc: Before Chickalettas playdate with Baby Yukiko, Mayor Goodway baby-proofs the playground, wrapping everything in rubbery Snap-N-Wrap, and gets stuck; Mayor Humdinger loses his voice.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppa and her family go on holiday in a special van. Peppa is surprised to find that at a push of a button the van turns into a little house for them to sleep in.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppa and her family are on holiday in their camper van. They decide to go to Duck Land, a special park where they can see the ducks in the river, and are surprised when their van turns into a boat.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppa and George take a bin of vegetable peelings to Grandpa Pigs compost heap. Worms inside the bin turn the peelings into compost, which Granny and Grandpa use to help their plants grow.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Richard Rabbit comes to play with George, while Suzy Sheep plays with Peppa. The boys dont want to play the same games as the girls, but they all agree that they like jumping up and down in muddy puddles.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Daddy Pig finds it hard work when he runs a race to raise money for the school roof. It is far easier to run when a wasp is chasing him.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Daddy Pig hangs his clean football shirt out to dry, but Peppa, George and Mummy splash it with mud. They put it in the wash, but Peppa puts her red dress in too - which turns Daddys shirt pink.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppa and George go on a boat trip with Grandpa Pig, but they must send Polly Parrot for help when their boat runs aground. Polly fetches Granny Pig and Granddad Dog to rescue them.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Delphine the Donkey comes to stay. Peppa takes Delphine to playgroup, where they teach her the `Bing Bong Song.

programme.title: Tim Rex In Space
programme.desc: Kai is super excited to ride the Nebulator, but Tim isnt so sure.

programme.title: Tim Rex In Space
programme.desc: Tim and Kai try to photograph the illusive SpaceBug.

programme.title: Tim Rex In Space
programme.desc: Tim, Tommy and Tia get lost at a play centre.

programme.title: Ben and Hollys Little Kingdom
programme.desc: Holly and her friends must stay in the courtyard and play with Hollys little twin sisters Daisy and Poppy. All is fine until the twins borrow a magic wand and escape from the castle.

programme.title: Ben and Hollys Little Kingdom
programme.desc: Queen Thistle has a beautiful new teapot that is so delicate that no one is allowed to touch it. Holly accidentally breaks the Queen Thistles teapot and tries to fix it with Bens help.

programme.title: Ben and Hollys Little Kingdom
programme.desc: Holly accidentally turns Ben into a frog. With the help of her friends, Holly tries to turn Ben back into an elf but instead turns him into a bird and then a rabbit.

programme.title: Ben and Hollys Little Kingdom
programme.desc: Holly spends the day with King Thistle on his royal duties. King Thistle must launch an elf boat, judge a fairy fruit competition and make a speech at the festival.

programme.title: The Adventures of Paddington
programme.sub-title: Paddington and the Caterpillar; Paddington Plays the Floor Is Lava
programme.desc: Paddington befriends a caterpillar; the Browns play a very competitive game of the Floor Is Lava.

programme.title: Gabbys Dollhouse
programme.desc: Its cousin time; Bakey, Flakey, Marshapan, and Paddycake visit Cakey and the dollhouse gang for a "sprinkle-rific" day of yummy snacks and hug attacks.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: When Mummy Pig helps out at Miss Rabbits fire station, she is called upon to douse the flames at Daddy Pigs out-of-control barbecue.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Granny and Grandpa Pig come for a dinner party with Mummy and Daddy Pig. Peppa and George are too tired to go to bed, until they are told a story.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppa brings home a teddy from playgroup to look after, but she is worried that he will not have a very exciting time.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: It is Danny Dogs birthday, and with the help of Grandad Dog and Grandpa Pig the children take part in a pirate treasure hunt.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: All the people in Peppas town are excited to meet Mr Potato when he comes to open a new sports centre.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Madame Gazelle takes Peppa and her friends on a train ride where they must tick off what they see on their activity sheets.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Granny Pig shows Peppa and George her poultry. Elsewhere, something has been eating Grandpa Pigs lettuces.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: At Peppas playgroup, Madame Gazelle wants everyone to show the class their special talent.

programme.title: Tim Rex In Space
programme.desc: Tim tries to find a new rocket thats perfect for him.

programme.title: Tim Rex In Space
programme.desc: Tim, Tommy and Tia find a lost mammoth in their house.

programme.title: Tim Rex In Space
programme.desc: Tim tries to be at two parties at once.

programme.title: Blues Clues and You!
programme.desc: Josh invites YOU to be a part of the construction crew! Blue and Magenta are building something special, and we need to play Blue and Magentas Clues to figure it out! While looking for clues, we fix and build things with our handy-dandy tools.

programme.title: Ben and Hollys Little Kingdom
programme.desc: Ben and Holly are having fun playing in the meadow. Holly gets frustrated when Ben uses his elf skills to win all the games, and Ben gets frustrated when Holly uses her magic skills.

programme.title: Ben and Hollys Little Kingdom
programme.desc: King Thistle has a cold and needs medicine. Nanny Plum, Holly and Ben have fun collecting the ingredients for a magic potion to make him better.

programme.title: Ben and Hollys Little Kingdom
programme.desc: Ben, Holly and Gaston the Ladybird find a large egg in the meadow. A tiny chick hatches from the egg and the children must find the mummy bird.

programme.title: Ben and Hollys Little Kingdom
programme.desc: Ben is in training for the elf games, Holly wants to join in but the rules state there is absolutely no flying or magic allowed. Ben gives Holly a chance to join in without using magic.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppas family go to the museums moon exhibition with Georges friend, Edmond Elephant.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Grandpa Pig takes Peppa and George to the playground, but does not understand the childrens rules.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Goldie the fish does not look happy and is not eating her food. Peppa, George, and Mummy Pig take her on a bus trip to visit Dr Hamster the vet.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppa and the family go to the funfair, but Mummy Pig is angry when people at the fair tell her that she will not be able to win prizes in the competitions.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppa and her friends learn how to count by playing games at Madame Gazelles playschool.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppa and her family are trying to get to the playground, but Mr Bulls roadworks are in the way.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppa and her friends are playing hide and seek, but it is Freddy Fox with his super smelling nose who is the best at playing that game.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppa tries to learn how to whistle after she realises that seemingly everyone but her can do it.

programme.title: The Adventures of Paddington
programme.sub-title: Paddingtons Space Adventure; Paddingtons Blackberry Adventure
programme.desc: Paddington takes off and becomes the worlds first Bearonaut; Jonathan, Judy, and Paddington pick all the blackberries in the garden, but Mr. Curry seems to think some are his.

programme.sub-title: Pups Save a Goldfish; Pups Save a Flying Florist
programme.desc: Uncle Otis accidentally slips and tosses Julia and Juliuss new goldfish, Marigold, into the river!When Ms. Marjorie and Mr. Park don wingsuits to see a rare flower, a flying squirrel sends them off course!

programme.sub-title: Katies Arctic Road Trip; Pups Save a Magical Mishap
programme.desc: Skye, Everest, Liberty, and Katie go on a camping adventure where they encounter a polar bear with a sweet tooth; when Julia and Julius put on a magic show, they are convinced they accidentally turned Mr. Carter into a rabbit.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Doctor Hamster visits the school and brings some animals with her. Binky the Tortoise is very popular - but ends up needing to be rescued by Miss Rabbit.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppa and her friends and family are looking forward to a trip to the seaside where they can make sandcastles, but when they arrive at the beach there is too much snow.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Mummy Pig gives her old computer to Granny and Grandpa Pig. They use it for lots of important work - as well as Happy Mrs Chicken, but who will fix it when it breaks?

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppa and her playgroup friends go with Madame Gazelle to visit Pedro Pony, who is poorly in hospital.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Grandpa Pig organises a chocolate egg hunt for Peppa and her friends. Will the piglets track down the treats?

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Miss Rabbit gives Mummy Pig, Peppa and George a ride in her rescue helicopter. Daddy Pig is very sad to escape all the fun - but Miss Rabbit makes sure that doesnt happen.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Baby Alexander is too young to talk, until Peppa teaches him his first word.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Grandpa Pig takes Peppa, George and Danny Dog to visit Grampy Rabbits lighthouse.

programme.title: American Monster
programme.desc: Family comes first to grandparents Ron and Kay Wilkson, though they may not have much money, their growing brood always knows how to make their own fun. However, something evil is prowling close to home and not everyone will make it out alive.

programme.title: Evil Lives Here

programme.title: Judge Rinders Crime Stories
programme.sub-title: Michelle Stewart; John Wilson; Helen Hill

But ex-boyfriend John Wilson had other ideas, failing to accept the break-up. He wanted to make sure that if he couldnt have Michelle, nor could anyone else. When young mum Helen Hills husband didnt come home after a night out, panic set in, but nothing could have prepared her for the truth behind his absence.

programme.title: The CEO Killer: Deny, Defend, Depose

programme.title: Julian Assange: Free at Last
programme.desc: WikiLeaks, founded in 2006 by Julian Assange, became famous for exposing hidden secrets. Assanges 2019 arrest after years of asylum led to his imprisonment and espionage charges in the US, sparking global debates on press freedom, until his release in June 2024.

programme.title: Who Killed JFK? The Conspiracies
programme.desc: John Fitzgerald Kennedys assassination is a memory etched onto the minds of most Americans. An exploration of the many theories that have arisen and sought to explain why the president was assassinated.

programme.title: Who Killed Gandhi?
programme.sub-title: Symbol of India Past and Future
programme.desc: The paths of three men crossed during these volatile times, resulting in a violent murder that would change the course of history. Each was dedicated to an independent mother India but separated by diametrically opposed views that would lead to an epic battle for the heart and soul of a nation.

programme.title: Tupac: Cover-Up
programme.desc: Tupac Shakur signs with Death Row Records and realises (perhaps too late) that all is not as it seems with the label.

programme.title: Tupac: Cover-Up
programme.desc: Los Angeles Police and Compton Police begin to advocate to Las Vegas Police that the shooting is a Compton Problem.

programme.title: Tupac: Cover-Up

programme.title: Tupac: Cover-Up

programme.title: Tupac: Cover-Up

programme.title: Wild Alpine Rivers
programme.desc: What would happen to the mountains without water? They would turn into deserts, devoid of life and vegetation. But there are very few mountains without water on our planet.

programme.title: Germanys Mystic Forest
programme.desc: 2.000 rivers and streams dig deep into the underground and transport their water into reservoirs or Germanys Wild Amazon, the Wupper. These forests and rivers, together with heathlands and moors, are home to a diverse fauna. Martens, badgers, wild boars, hares, roe deer, and red deer, even black grouse and hazel deer find shelter in these parts. Wolves also have a dominant presence, not to mention those who live underground, such as lizards, bats, and snakes.

programme.title: Lionfish: The New Pirates of the Caribbean
programme.desc: Armed with 18 venomous spines, the lionfish hunts, invisible to its prey, in packs. Shedding light on the fascinating behaviour that let the lionfish conquer foreign waters, and by doing so threaten entire ecosystems.

programme.title: Animal Armoury
programme.desc: Colouring that matches or blends with their surroundings helps the Arctic fox, green tree frog and penguin hide from predators.

programme.title: Animal Armoury
programme.desc: Many animals such as toads, moths, snakes and spiders have poison glands, while the spines of the stone fish and the scorpion fish are poisonous.

programme.title: Lions On The Move
programme.desc: Challenging two part documentary with Kevin Richardson, looking at the challenges of moving 42 big cats to a new home in South Africa.

programme.title: Big Cats of the Serengeti
programme.desc: Cameras reveal the last secrets of Africas largest wild lions by following two prides.

programme.title: Megeti - Africas Lost Wolf
programme.desc: The story of the Ethiopian she-wolf Megeti, who lost her pack and is suddenly completely on her own. Since it is essential for the survival of the young wolf to rejoin a pack, she wanders through the Ethiopian highlands in search of a new home.

programme.title: Baboon Bandits
programme.desc: A close look at the Table Mountain baboons and the local reactions they incite.

programme.title: Wild Moments
programme.desc: Features geckos, screech owls, penguins of warmer climates, and building a playground for squirrels.

programme.title: Wild Faces of Switzerland
programme.desc: Learn about four different regions of Switzerland. From the rugged mountains of the Ticino High Alps to the lovely Muggio Valley in the southernmost corner of Ticino, hardly any other canton is so full of contrasts.

programme.title: Wild Faces of Switzerland
programme.desc: The river called Aare is the only body of water that runs through Switzerland. It rises from a source in the Bernese Alps and finally flows into the Rhine. The Aare offers important habitat for flora and fauna.

programme.title: Of Shark and Man
programme.desc: "Of Shark and Man" is a ground breaking film about one mans journey to get closer than anyone thought possible, to the worlds biggest Bull Sharks and tell the incredible untold story of Shark Reef in Fiji, one of the greatest marine conservation successes of all time. A love letter to the worlds most feared predator, in which the shark is the hero.

programme.title: Lioness in Exile
programme.desc: Manyari takes an unusual step when she takes her young cubs away from the sanctuary of the clan.

programme.title: Cheetah: Beating the Odds
programme.desc: A cheetah raises her young family on the vast expanse of the Serengeti, facing enormous challenges. Lions are quick to throw their muscle around, and hyenas and jackals attempt to steal anything the cheetah manages to kill.

programme.title: Giant Sea Serpent: Meet The Myth
programme.desc: Scientific buoys offer a new perspective on the giant oarfish, the planets largest bony fish and a possible source for legends of sea serpents.

programme.title: Animal Armoury
programme.desc: The archer fish spits water from a `blowgun in its mouth to capture bugs, while the jawfish spits pebbles from its mouth to defend itself.

programme.title: Animal Armoury

programme.title: Coral Sea Dreaming

programme.desc: Todays house hunt is in North Wales where Jules Hudson is helping a pair of returning expats find their ideal country home for a budget of £350,000. Whilst in the region, Jules heads out with a local fisherman to gather wild mussels using an ancient method thats been used on Wales northern coast for 200 years.

programme.title: Salvage Hunters
programme.desc: Drew and Alister hunt for pieces inspired by nature, from a folk-art birds head walking stick in Brighton to a fireside tiger in Shropshire.

programme.title: Salvage Hunters
programme.desc: Modern day treasure hunter Drew Pritchard travels the length and breadth of the UK in search of weird and wonderful objects. The bigger the hoard, the happier he is.

programme.title: Salvage Hunters

programme.title: The Yorkshire Auction House
programme.desc: Auctioneer Angus Ashworth and his team perform house clearances and uncover hidden gems. What will these antiques sell for at auction?

programme.title: The Yorkshire Auction House
programme.desc: Auctioneer Angus Ashworth and his team perform house clearances and uncover hidden gems. What will these antiques sell for at auction?

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Upholster Sonnaz Nooranvary and metal expert Dominic Chinea join forces to revive a Victorian iron rocking chair that has given great comfort to a family for generations. Current custodian Katie looked after her ailing father in the chair, and since he passed away it means more to her than ever. Mechanically minded David Burville takes on the fix of a quirky mechanical bear ornament.

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Margie Cooper and Paul Martin head for Plymouth on the final leg of their journey. The competition has tightened, and only a few pounds separate the rivals. Along the way, Margie hears the story of a footballer, once forgotten but finally being given the recognition he deserves. And Paul is all at sea discovering the tale of a lighthouse, built in the 18th century, that became a beacon of engineering prowess.

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Antiques experts travel across the nation to search for hidden treasures, from household items to one-of-a kind collectibles. The experts then head to auction to battle it out and make the biggest profit from their items.

programme.title: Long Lost Family: What Happened Next
programme.desc: Following reunited siblings Geoffrey Tonks and Barbara Jacobs as they investigate a family mystery and try to trace a missing sister. Geoffrey was the product of a war-time affair and spent his early years in Barnardos. Before Barbara asked the `Long Lost Family team to find him, he had never met a single blood relative and his unhappy childhood meant he had no desire to learn about his past.

programme.title: Long Lost Family: What Happened Next
programme.desc: We follow three stories of sons coming to terms with the past. Our first story catches up with Marc Wolfe, who we reunited with his birth mother Esther in 2017. Unfortunately, we find out that they have subsequently struggled to form a bond. Our second story was too delicate to share on television until now. In 2017 Sheila Knox came to us desperate to find the baby she gave up for adoption when she was a 15-year-old schoolgirl.

programme.title: The Yorkshire Auction House
programme.desc: Auctioneer Angus Ashworth and his team are called out to peoples homes with a mission to sift through a lifetime of possessions and find forgotten treasures that can be sold at auction.

programme.title: The Yorkshire Auction House
programme.desc: Angus helps a man sell his siblings rare books and toys. Plus, a cruise ship singer wants to downsize her kitsch collection of mid-century ornaments.

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Comedian Chris Ramsey draws huge audiences to his live comedy shows and also presents a podcast and TV show with his wife Rosie about the trials and tribulations of being married with children. From a South Shields working-class family, Chris feels he has a charmed and lucky life and wonders if his ancestors were as fortunate. He starts by investigating his great-grandad Dryden Gordon Young, who fought in the First World War.

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Sports presenter and former footballer Gary Lineker follows the trail of his great, great great grandfather James Pratt, and discovers that James was a poacher in Victorian England and served time in Leicester Prison. Gary wants to know why James ended up becoming a repeat offender.

programme.title: Help! My House is Haunted
programme.desc: Three years ago, entrepreneur David bought a 33-acre former Napoleonic fortress, but hes been plagued by paranormal activity ever since. The team take on the largest location theyve ever investigated in order to restore harmony to Davids home.

programme.title: Help! My House is Haunted
programme.desc: Geraldines 13th-century Bardon Manor in Somerset is plagued by spirits, especially in the sinister East Wing. The team are tested to their limits as they work to uncover what lurks within the ancient walls.

programme.title: Help! My House is Haunted
programme.desc: Alice and her family moved into their Barnsley home two years ago, only to be plagued by terrifying paranormal activity. The team unearths a mining explosion that killed over 300, with some bodies never discovered.

programme.desc: Drew and Al trawl collections curated by experts, unearthing a rare item of motoring history and a 19th-century model ship with a tale to tell.

programme.title: Portrait Artist of the Year
programme.desc: Who is the best portrait artist in the UK and Ireland? Thats the question that this programme tries to answer. The show, with its judging panel, travels to London, Glasgow, Dublin and Cardiff to hold competitions to find each citys top portrait artist. Those four artists then travel to Paris for further inspiration and development, producing one further work during their two days in Frances capital city. The four finalists work is then displayed in Londons National Portrait Gallery for a period. In addition to being considered for further projects by the gallery, the winner is awarded a £10,000 commission to produce a portrait of author Hilary Mantel that will become part of The British Librarys permanent art collection.

programme.desc: Explore the charming and complex career of British actor Hugh Grant, whose wit, charisma, and vulnerability made him a defining figure in romantic comedies. Delve into Grants ability to capture the nuances of love, relationships, and connection.

programme.title: Luther: Never Too Much
programme.desc: The captivating story of Luther Vandross, from his early Sesame Street appearances to becoming the indisputable master of the love song. Using rare archives, Vandross shares his own story with insights from his closest collaborators.

programme.title: Indiana Jones and the Temple of Doom
programme.desc: Adventure sequel. Intrepid archaeologist Indiana Jones, on the trail of fortune and glory in Old Shanghai, is ricocheted into a dangerous adventure in India. With his faithful companion Short Round and nightclub singer Willie Scott, Indie goes in search of the magical Sankara stone, and uncovers an ancient evil which threatens all who come into contact with it.

programme.title: The Italian Job
programme.desc: On an earlier job, one of Charlie Crokers gang steals millions of dollars worth of gold from the rest, so they devise a brilliant and complex plan to steal the bullion back. This time theyre going to need a new safe cracker, and Charlie approaches the beautiful Stella, a safe expert who has never worked on the wrong side of the law.

programme.title: Demolition Man
programme.desc: Violence has been outlawed in the politically correct regime of 2032, and the only weapons in existence are consigned to a museum, but when an escaped psychopath heads straight for the museum, the authorities decide the only effective recourse they have is to thaw out a violent, cryogenically frozen cop.

programme.title: Venom
programme.desc: Journalist Eddie Brock is trying to take down Carlton Drake, the notorious and brilliant founder of the Life Foundation. While investigating one of Drakes experiments, Eddies body merges with the alien Venom -- leaving him with superhuman strength and power. Twisted, dark and fueled by rage, Venom tries to control the new and dangerous abilities that Eddie finds so intoxicating.

programme.title: The Patriot
programme.desc: A widowed farmer, with a brave but brutal military past, decides not to join up when the British arrive in 1776. However, when his son enlists and is later captured by the enemy, the former soldier must abandon his new-found pacifist principles in order to rescue his oldest child, and forms a regiment of Carolina patriots, whose guerrilla tactics prove pivotal to the US war effort.

programme.title: Spider-Man: No Way Home
programme.desc: Spider-Mans identity is revealed to everyone, and he can no longer separate his normal life from his superhero life. When he asks Doctor Strange for help, it forces him to discover what it means to be him.

programme.title: Indiana Jones and the Last Crusade
programme.desc: The intrepid explorer Indiana Jones sets out to rescue his father, a medievalist who has vanished while searching for the Holy Grail. Following clues in the old mans notebook, Indy arrives in Venice, where he enlists the help of a beautiful academic, but they are not the only ones who are on the trail, and some sinister old enemies soon come out of the woodwork.

programme.title: Indiana Jones and the Kingdom of the Crystal Skull
programme.desc: Professor Henry Jones is given a coded message during the Cold War which leads him to Peru in search of a legendary skull. Jones needs to deliver the skull to its rightful place, but Soviet agents who are trailing Jones have other plans which will endanger the West if the artefact falls into their hands.

programme.title: Kong: Skull Island
programme.desc: Scientists, soldiers and adventurers encounter the mighty Kong and other monstrous creatures after travelling to an uncharted island in the Pacific Ocean.

programme.title: Mission: Impossible - The Final Reckoning
programme.desc: Ethan Hunt and the IMF team race against time to find the Entity, a rogue artificial intelligence that can destroy mankind.

programme.title: Hounds of War
programme.desc: A mercenary seeks revenge after his entire team gets wiped out during a final mission.

programme.title: Love Hurts
programme.desc: Marvin is a Milwaukee realtor who receives a crimson envelope from a former partner-in-crime whom he left for dead. He now finds himself thrust back into a world of ruthless hit men and double-crosses that turn his open houses into deadly war zones.

programme.title: The Fast and the Furious
programme.desc: An undercover cop infiltrates a gang of LA street racers while investigating a rash of truck robberies.

programme.title: The Fast and the Furious
programme.desc: An undercover cop infiltrates a gang of LA street racers while investigating a rash of truck robberies.

programme.title: The Sloth Lane
programme.desc: A sloth named Laura and her family move to the big city with nothing but their prized family recipe book and a rusted old food truck. The familys delicious food soon catches the eye of a quick-witted cheetah named Dotti.

programme.title: Into the Wonderwoods
programme.desc: Ten-year-old Angelo, an aspiring explorer, is left behind by his distracted parents at a rest stop. He then ventures into a forest inhabited by strange, wonderful creatures to find them.

programme.title: Riverdance
programme.desc: An Irish boy and a Spanish girl journey into the mythical world of the Megaloceros Giganteus who teach them to appreciate Riverdance as a celebration of life.

programme.title: Fireheart
programme.desc: In New York City in 1932, women are not allowed to be firefighters. When the citys firemen disappear in mysterious fires, Georgia Nolan sees a golden opportunity. She disguises herself as Joe and joins a team of improvised firemen.

programme.title: Shrek 2
programme.desc: Ogre Shrek and his new wife Fiona are surprised to discover a letter from her parents, inviting them over for dinner. Fiona is eager to show off her new husband, but her parents dont yet know shes an ogre. Taking Donkey along, they set off for Far Far Away to visit them, but the trip is hampered by the jealousy of a wicked fairy godmother and her hired assassin, Puss in Boots.

programme.title: Shrek the Third
programme.desc: Shrek is just starting to settle down in his beloved swamp with his wife Fiona, but their mucky bliss doesnt last for long as word arrives that the ogres froggy father-in-law is about to croak. The very last thing Shrek wants is to become King, but with no other heir in sight, it looks like the grumpy green one is destined never to live quietly in the swamp again. A glimmer of hope appears in the form of heir Artie.

programme.title: DC League of Super-Pets
programme.desc: Krypto the Super-Dog and Superman are inseparable best friends, now Krypto must convince a ragtag group of animals to master their own newfound powers to help him save Superman and the rest of the Justice League.

programme.title: Hotel Transylvania
programme.desc: Dracula, who operates a high-end resort away from the human world, goes into overprotective mode when a boy discovers the resort and falls for the counts teen-aged daughter.

programme.title: Flushed Away
programme.desc: A spoiled rat gets flushed down the toilet and finds himself stranded in Londons sewer underworld. A savvy scavenger helps him evade frog assassins and make his way back to his plush home.

programme.desc: A life-saving surgery fuses together a police officer and his faithful police dog -- and Dog Man is born. Learning to embrace his new identity, Dog Man must stop feline supervillain Petey the Cat from cloning himself and going on a crime spree.
